children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out the programs established under sections 16154 and 16161a of this title, the Secretary shall award multiyear grants to, and enter into contracts, cooperative agreements, or any other agreements authorized under this Act or other Federal law with, eligible entities (as determined by the Secretary) for research, development, and demonstration projects to advance new clean hydrogen production, processing, delivery, storage, and use equipment manufacturing technologies and techniques.

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of resources, including existing energy infrastructure;

children.label: B
children.text: support domestic supply chains for materials and components;

children.label: C
children.text: identify and incorporate nonhazardous alternative materials for components and devices;

children.label: D
children.text: operate in partnership with tribal energy development organizations, Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, Native Hawaiian community-based organizations, or territories or freely associated States; or

children.label: E
children.text: are located in economically distressed areas of the major natural gas-producing regions of the United States.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 3 Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , and not less frequently than once every 4 years thereafter, the Secretary shall conduct, and make available to the public and the relevant committees of Congress, an independent review of the progress of the projects carried out through grants awarded, or contracts, cooperative agreements, or other agreements entered into, under paragraph (1).November 15, 2021

children.heading: Clean hydrogen technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
children.label: b Clean hydrogen technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
A
children.text: increasing the efficiency and cost-effectiveness of the recovery of raw materials from clean hydrogen technology components and systems, including enabling technologies such as electrolyzers and fuel cells;

children.label: B
children.text: minimizing environmental impacts from the recovery and disposal processes;

children.label: C
children.text: addressing any barriers to the research, development, demonstration, and commercialization of technologies and processes for the disassembly and recycling of devices used for clean hydrogen production, processing, delivery, storage, and use;

children.label: D
children.text: developing alternative materials, designs, manufacturing processes, and other aspects of clean hydrogen technologies;

children.label: E
children.text: developing alternative disassembly and resource recovery processes that enable efficient, cost-effective, and environmentally responsible disassembly of, and resource recovery from, clean hydrogen technologies; and

children.label: F
children.text: developing strategies to increase consumer acceptance of, and participation in, the recycling of fuel cells.

children.heading: Dissemination of results
children.label: 2 Dissemination of results
children.text: The Secretary shall make available to the public and the relevant committees of Congress the results of the projects carried out through grants awarded, or contracts, cooperative agreements, or other agreements entered into, under paragraph (1), including any educational and outreach materials developed by the projects.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: c Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $500,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
